item.title: ImageLoader: in-flight Task.detached loads not cancelled on dealloc — remote image tasks outlive the loader
item.intent: Network requests must not outlive the component that issued them — dangling URLSession tasks waste bandwidth and complicate memory analysis
item.description: loadImageIfNeeded fires Task.detached without storing the Task handle. When ImageLoader is deallocated (e.g., document closed), any in-flight URLSession downloads for remote images continue running with no way to cancel them. The [weak self] guard prevents crashes, but the URLSession download itself is not cancelled, wasting bandwidth for potentially large remote images.
steps: In Sources/EMEditor/ImageLoader.swift, add private var tasks: [URL: Task<Void, Never>] = [:] to the class
In loadImageIfNeeded, change 'Task.detached { ... }' to 'let task = Task.detached { ... }' and store: tasks[url] = task
Inside the Task body in the MainActor.run block, after setting cache entry, add: self.tasks.removeValue(forKey: url)
Add deinit to ImageLoader: tasks.values.forEach { $0.cancel() }; tasks.removeAll()
acceptanceCriteria: Deallocating ImageLoader cancels all stored in-flight tasks
Task handle is removed from tasks dictionary when load completes (no unbounded growth)
Existing load-deduplication via inFlightURLs is unaffected
